mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls and declining unemployment signals strong labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings visibility, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to predict SPX returns with a 20-day IC < 0.05 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production indicates robust manufacturing activity that supports corporate profit growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in high INDPRO growth periods show no significant outperformance relative to baseline (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized expansion in residential construction and consumer spending signals strong aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined HOUST-RSAFS signal fails to predict SPX 5-day returns with a 5-day IC < 0.10 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2% triggers central bank tightening expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in high-inflation periods show no significant underperformance relative to baseline (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in GDP growth below 1% signals weakening economic fundamentals that depress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in low-GDP growth periods show no significant underperformance relative to baseline (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment above 6% indicates deteriorating labor market conditions that lower consumer confidence and corporate earnings forecasts, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in high-unemployment periods show no significant underperformance relative to baseline (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production below 100 signals weakening manufacturing activity that reduces corporate profitability, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in low-INDPRO periods show no significant underperformance relative to baseline (t-test p > 0.10).
